objectives.text: Listen for specific information and respond appropriately in oral activities.
Speak clearly to describe experiences, retell events, and share ideas.
Ask and answer simple questions to clarify meaning and extend discussion.
Identify letter-sound relationships and use them to decode simple words.
Blend and segment sounds in one- and two-syllable words.
Use grade-appropriate sight words and context clues to build vocabulary.
Identify key details, main ideas, and story elements in simple texts.
Make predictions and simple inferences based on what is read.
Respond to fiction and nonfiction texts using personal connections and questions.
Write simple sentences and short responses using appropriate conventions.
Use basic grammar, punctuation, and capitalization in written work.
Organize ideas clearly in short written tasks for different purposes.
Read, write, compare, and order numbers appropriate to the grade level.
Use place value understanding to represent and explain numbers.
Solve simple number problems using mental and written strategies.
Use addition and subtraction accurately in everyday problems.
Develop multiplication and division facts and use them in simple tasks.
Explain strategies used to solve number sentences and word problems.
Estimate and measure using standard units appropriate to the task.
Read and record time and simple measurements accurately.
Choose appropriate units and tools for measuring real objects.
Identify and describe common 2D and 3D shapes and their features.
Create and extend simple number and shape patterns.
Read and interpret simple pictographs, bar graphs, or tables.
Describe self, family roles, and responsibilities in the community.
Show respect for self and others in classroom and community situations.
Participate in simple projects about family, school, and community life.
Observe and describe features of the local environment.
Explain how people, plants, and animals depend on each other.
Use simple inquiry and project work to solve familiar real-world problems.
Identify habits that support personal health, hygiene, and safety.
Demonstrate positive social skills and respectful relationships.
Discuss feelings, choices, and behaviours that promote well-being.
Read texts independently and identify main ideas, supporting details, and purpose.
Write clear paragraphs and short compositions for different purposes.
Use grammar, spelling, and punctuation more accurately in oral and written communication.

topics.name: Vocabulary, Fluency, and Response to Text

objectives.text: Use vocabulary, context clues, and word analysis to understand text.
Read with growing fluency, expression, and confidence.
Respond critically and personally to fiction and nonfiction texts.
Solve problems involving whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.
Use number properties, factors, and multiples to explain relationships.
Select efficient strategies for computation and checking answers.
Estimate, measure, and calculate quantities using appropriate units.
Find perimeter and area of common shapes and interpret results.
Identify, describe, and compare angles, lines, and geometric figures.

topics.name: Patterns, Algebraic Thinking, and Data Handling

objectives.text: Describe and extend number or geometric patterns using rules.
Represent simple relationships using algebraic expressions or equations.
Collect, organize, and interpret data using tables and graphs.
Classify living things and describe how they interact with their environment.
Investigate structures and functions that help organisms survive.
Explain how environmental changes affect living things.

Investigate forces, motion, and energy in everyday situations.
Explain basic Earth and weather processes using evidence.

Examine choices and behaviours that affect self and others.

Work cooperatively in games and fitness tasks.

topics.name: Algebra, Graphs, Geometry, Statistics, and Probability

objectives.text: Solve algebraic, geometric, trigonometric, and graphical problems accurately.
Interpret and represent data using appropriate statistical methods.
Select and evaluate methods for solving unfamiliar mathematical problems.
Interpret spoken, written, and visual texts critically and effectively.
Write organized responses for informative, persuasive, and literary purposes.
Use language appropriately for audience, purpose, and context.
Explain interactions among organisms and their environment.
Analyze ecological relationships using data, diagrams, and real-world examples.
Evaluate human impact on ecosystems and biodiversity.

topics.name: Cells, Nutrition, Respiration, Transport, and Health

objectives.text: Explain major life processes and the structures that support them.
Interpret biological data and experiments related to life processes.
Apply biological knowledge to health and disease contexts.
Explain reproduction and inheritance using appropriate biological terms.
Analyze patterns of inheritance and variation.
Evaluate the importance of genetics in living organisms and society.
Explain chemical structure, bonding, and reactions using scientific principles.
Apply chemical calculations and equations to solve structured problems.
Interpret practical and theoretical chemical information accurately.
Describe important organic and inorganic compounds and their properties.
Relate chemical principles to industry, daily life, and the environment.
Use practical evidence to explain chemical behaviour and changes.

topics.name: Mechanics, Thermal Physics, Waves, Electricity, and Atomic Physics

objectives.text: Apply physical principles to explain motion, energy, waves, and electrical systems.
Interpret graphs, experiments, and calculations in physics contexts.
Evaluate physical evidence and communicate scientific reasoning clearly.
Explain major scientific ideas related to organisms and life processes.
Apply inquiry skills to investigate biological and health-related situations.
Interpret evidence and scientific information in everyday contexts.
Explain scientific concepts related to energy, forces, matter, and the environment.
Use scientific investigations and data to solve everyday problems.
Evaluate environmental and technological issues using evidence.
Analyze social relationships, institutions, and governance in Caribbean contexts.
Apply social studies concepts to everyday issues and decision-making.
Evaluate social problems and propose informed responses.

frameworks.subject: Social Studies
frameworks.topic_group: Sustainable Development and Use of Resources

objectives.text: Explain sustainable development and the use of resources in the Caribbean.
Interpret information about development, resources, and regional issues.
Evaluate policies and actions related to resource use and regional growth.

topics.name: Computer Fundamentals, Networks, and Social Impact

objectives.text: Explain computer systems, networks, and the social impact of information technology.
Use IT concepts to solve practical and communication problems.
Evaluate responsible and secure use of digital technologies.
Create solutions using productivity tools, databases, and web technologies.
Design and implement simple algorithms or programs to solve problems.
Interpret user needs and select appropriate technological solutions.
